name: Ned Land
description: Ned Land, the Canadian harpooner, is a rugged study in earthy tones and weathered textures in this lush watercolor world. His face, framed by a mop of roughly cut, ginger-red hair streaked with sun-bleached blonde, is a canvas of windburn and sea-salt. Freckles, like scattered bronze coins, dot his high cheekbones, which are sharply defined beneath piercing, cobalt-blue eyes that constantly scan the horizon. A thick, unruly ginger beard, flecked with silver at the edges, obscures the lower half of his face, but hints at a strong, determined jaw. He favors practical, if slightly mismatched, clothing: a worn, dark indigo oilskin jacket layered over a faded ochre fisherman's sweater, its thick wool rendered in soft, textural brushstrokes. Wide-legged, patched-up canvas trousers in a muted sage green are tucked into knee-high, well-worn leather boots, painted with the same careful attention to detail that defines his face, reflecting the grit and determination of a life spent wrestling with the ocean. The overall palette leans towards a blend of cool blues and greens contrasting with warm reds and browns, emphasizing his connection to both the sea and the land.
